summary: Catalog #117 (check_subagent_commit_serializer_uses_lock) has hash-prefix matching bug. Compares short_sha (7 chars) against seen_hashes (which contains 9-char prefixes from serializer-log head_after field). Neither short-vs-9 nor full-vs-9 ever matches. Result: every commit in serializer log reported as false-positive violation. Gate has been STRUCTURALLY BROKEN since landing.
fix_recommendation: Change `if short_sha in seen_hashes or full_sha in seen_hashes` to `if any(seen.startswith(short_sha) or full_sha.startswith(seen) for seen in seen_hashes)` (~2 LOC). Add 3 dedicated tests (positive 7-vs-9-char match, negative 7-vs-7-char no-match, multi-prefix-collision boundary). Re-run #117 and re-evaluate the 50-violation count.

summary: Catalog #233 4-gate canonical token-substring matching has structural false-positive class. A lane evidence text mentioning '100ep' in any context (e.g. 'discussion of 100ep vs 200ep tradeoffs') trips gate 3 (auth_eval_100ep) false-positive. Empirically verified: text 'See discussion of 100ep vs 200ep tradeoffs in the smoke green report; tier c was measured; validate_custody verified; evidence_grade=contest_cuda' returns (True, True, True, True) — all 4 gates PASS on prose-only. Sister bug class to Catalog #136 which already extincted this exact pattern for ANOTHER token set; #233's tokens were added without inheriting #136's structural-validation discipline.
fix_recommendation: Refactor to require structured-field declarations (e.g. gate_evidence:{auth_eval_anchor:{archive_sha256:..., score:..., axis:...}}) instead of substring matching. Pattern: Catalog #136's _CUSTODY_VALIDATOR_TOKENS filtered through AST-aware structural validation. Add sister gate check_catalog_233_4_gate_no_prose_token_overlap that flags any token <8 chars OR substring-of-another-gate-token.

summary: D9 FALLBACK_PROVIDERS_PER_CLASS is semantically overloaded. Time-Traveler amendment fires when fallback is >25% CHEAPER than canonical (Vast.ai RTX_4090 fallback to Modal A100 for 'full' class works correctly). But long_burn fallback is Vast.ai H100 ($1.50-1.99/hr) vs canonical Lightning A100 (operator subscription, $0/hr). H100 is MORE expensive — Time-Traveler amendment will NEVER fire for long_burn. The canonical doc per_class_provider_routing_canonical_20260514.md Section 3 confirms the long_burn fallback semantic is 'race-mode urgency (Lightning A100 saturated)' — capacity-overflow, not cheaper-alternative. Two conflicting semantics in the SAME data structure with no semantic discriminator.
fix_recommendation: Council-grade decision per CLAUDE.md 'Design decisions — non-negotiable'. Recommended Option B: tag each fallback tuple with FallbackReason enum (CHEAPER_ALTERNATIVE | CAPACITY_OVERFLOW). Add tests asserting Time-Traveler amendment never fires when fallback is more expensive. Update canonical doc to remove the conflict.

summary: STRATEGIC: Wave A added 8463 LOC across 5 commits in 9-minute window (15:53:02 -> 16:02:03 UTC), with 0 contest-CUDA empirical anchors landing in same window. .omx/state/RACE_MODE_ACTIVE.flag exists. Per CLAUDE.md 'Race-mode rigor inversion + parallel-dispatch first' non-negotiable HIGHEST EMPHASIS: 'If RACE_MODE_ACTIVE.flag exists, campaign actuation outranks new grand-council text unless that text directly writes launchable commands.' Wave A is exactly the May 4 race postmortem failure pattern: building infrastructure (per-class routing, 4-gate canonical, latent-replacement grammar, MDL extension, autopilot halve) instead of dispatching to harvest empirical anchors. Sister of CLAUDE.md 'Forbidden empirical-claim-without-evidence-tag' applied at strategic level: 5 PROCEED unanimous decisions with NO empirical anchor in same window.
fix_recommendation: Operator pivots from infrastructure-build mode to dispatch mode IMMEDIATELY. Per the race-mode non-negotiable, fan out Z5/C1 contest-scale Modal A100 + at least 2 sister substrate full-runs in next 2-hour window. Defer further council omnibus until >=3 contest-CUDA anchors land. This is a STRATEGIC finding, not a code finding — each commit is independently CLEAN; the issue is the 5-commit landing cadence during active race mode.

summary: Z3 v1 (archive.py 16.2KB + inflate.py 5.3KB + score_aware_loss.py 8.1KB) still LIVE despite commit message claim 'v1 retired as redundant per same verdict'. v1 surfaces remain exported via __init__.py lines 32-46 + are imported by trainer (experiments/train_substrate_z3_balle_hyperprior_bolton.py:119). v2 is OPT-IN behind --enable-v2-latent-replacement flag; v1 IS the production default. Documentation-vs-reality drift in commit message.
fix_recommendation: Either (A) update commit message language to 'v1 PRESERVED as reference; v2 is the score-affecting path', (B) actually retire v1 by removing exports + updating trainer to v2-only, OR (C) add a deprecation warning to v1's __init__.py exports. Per CLAUDE.md 'Beauty, simplicity': delete dead fields, stale adapters, and duplicate one-offs once a canonical contract replaces them.

summary: vq_vae F3 wire-in lacks cached-vs-uncached equivalence test. The PDP F3 wire-in has sister test test_pdp_loss_cache_path_equivalent_to_gt_forward asserting cached path produces byte-equivalent loss to uncached GT-forward path. This catches the cache-key bug class explicitly raised in council Decision 2 deliberation: 'cache HIT with wrong key -> silently wrong score'. vq_vae F3 wire-in tests in src/tac/tests/test_f3_backport_vqvae_pdp_wired.py are TEXT-MATCHING ONLY (test_vq_vae_calls_gt_cache_lookup_in_hot_loop checks token count). NO equivalent of test_vq_vae_loss_cache_path_equivalent_to_gt_forward. The cache-key bug class is unguarded for vq_vae.
fix_recommendation: Add test_vq_vae_loss_cache_path_equivalent_to_gt_forward (~30 LOC) mirroring the PDP test. Per CLAUDE.md 'Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against': sister-pattern protection across F3 surfaces. The cache-key bug class is the same on both surfaces.

summary: Tier C per-tensor std noise scaling assumes Gaussian distribution. Code: 'rel_std = v_dev.std().clamp(min=1e-8); noise = torch.randn_like(v_dev) * (rel_std * sigma)'. For substrates with sparse/long-tail weight distributions (DP1 coordinate-MLP at hidden=64 has very different shape than A1 HNeRV decoder at 162KB), per-tensor std UNDERESTIMATES the perturbation needed to reach fixed fractional information loss. Cross-substrate Tier C density comparison (e.g. PR106 density 0.95 vs DP1 density 0.45) is unreliable when underlying weight distribution shape varies.
fix_recommendation: Add noise_scaling_method parameter to _run_tier_c_* (default 'gaussian_std' for back-compat; alternatives 'mad_robust' = sigma * MAD(W) * 1.4826, 'iqr_robust' = sigma * IQR(W) / 1.349). Document cross-substrate comparison caveat in Tier C result schemas.

summary: DP1 Tier C reconstructs renderer from scratch per pair. _run_tier_c_dp1 calls 'DrivingPriorRenderer(cfg).to(device).eval()' inside _render_pair_with_residual which is invoked once per pair per sigma per perturbation target. For 600 pairs * 4 sigmas * 2 targets = 4800 renderer constructions per Tier C run. Empirical: 1.0s wall-clock on macOS CPU is fine, but on Modal A100 dispatch with 600-frame archives at sigma_count=10, this becomes 12000 constructor calls = ~2 min wasted overhead per Tier C invocation.
fix_recommendation: Refactor _render_pair_with_residual to construct renderer ONCE per _run_tier_c_dp1 call + call renderer.load_state_dict(perturbed_sd, strict=False) per pair. Sister pattern: _run_tier_c_a1 already does this (single decoder instance constructed once, state_dict swapped per perturbation).

summary: D9 classify_dispatch transition-zone boundary asymmetry. classify_dispatch uses '>=' for long_burn and '<=' for smoke. A borderline 12.0h dispatch routes to long_burn (Lightning A100, $50+ class) instead of full (Vast.ai 4090, $2-15 class) — 5-10x cost jump for 1-second wall-clock difference at boundary. Convex feasibility regions should have OPEN boundaries when crossing them changes cost class non-trivially.
fix_recommendation: Change '>=' to '>' for long_burn boundary check OR document the closed-boundary canonical decision in council ledger. The closed-boundary semantic is consistent with dispatch class definitions but creates discontinuous cost expectation.

summary: Catalog #233 token sets declared as tuple[str, ...] instead of frozenset[str]. Makes membership checks O(N) instead of O(1) and allows mutation in tests. Sister _CHECK_233_WAIVER_PLACEHOLDER_REJECTS correctly uses frozenset.
fix_recommendation: Convert the 4 token sets to frozenset[str] for O(1) membership and immutability. Per CLAUDE.md 'Beauty, simplicity': consistent use of immutable data structures.

Premise verification at .omx/tmp/r2_critical_premise_verifier.txt (rc=0): BOYD-1 confirmed empirically. FALLBACK_PROVIDERS_PER_CLASS['long_burn'] = [('vastai', 'H100')] vs canonical lightning/A100 ($0/hr subscription) — H100 is MORE expensive — Time-Traveler amendment will NEVER fire because cost-shift inequality fails. Capacity-overflow semantic had no code path. ProviderRoutingDecision exposes fallback_reason field + as_dict() serialization. Backwards-compat preserved via FALLBACK_PROVIDERS_PER_CLASS legacy alias (read-only union). MACKAY-2 (Catalog #233 token sets declared as tuple[str, ...] instead of frozenset[str]) was auto-closed by commit 3882468ef ("preflight: harden l1 promotion evidence matching") which converted all 4 cited token sets to frozenset as part of L1->L2 promotion evidence hardening. Verified at HEAD: _CHECK_233_SMOKE_GREEN_TOKENS, _CHECK_233_TIER_C_TOKENS, _CHECK_233_AUTH_EVAL_100EP_TOKENS, _CHECK_233_CUSTODY_VALIDATOR_TOKENS all declared as frozenset[str]. No new self-protection gate needed — frozenset hygiene is mathematical/idiomatic, not a recurring bug class. R2-LOW subagent verified premise and skipped re-fix per CLAUDE.md "Premise verification before edit" pattern.

BOYD-2 (D9 classify_dispatch transition-zone boundary asymmetry) closed by changing the long_burn upgrade boundaries from CLOSED >= to OPEN > on lines 1208 (wallclock) and 1220 (cost) of src/tac/cost_band_calibration.py. Per Boyd convex-feasibility lens: regions whose boundary crossing changes the cost class non-trivially MUST use OPEN boundaries. At exactly 12.0h or 100 USD, the safer routing is now "full" (-15 class) instead of "long_burn" ($50+ class) — operators must explicitly request long_burn. Smoke downgrade boundaries kept CLOSED <= because they route to the cheaper class (no cost penalty for over-routing). Self-protection: new Catalog #239 STRICT preflight gate check_classify_dispatch_no_raw_ge_boundaries refuses any future state of classify_dispatch using >= against PER_CLASS_SOFT_WALLCLOCK_CEILING_HR or PER_CLASS_SOFT_COST_CEILING_USD indexed by an UPGRADE class (full/long_burn/eval). Same-line waiver # CLASSIFY_DISPATCH_GE_BOUNDARY_OK:<rationale> for the rare future case (placeholder rejected). Wired strict=True from byte one per Strict-flip atomicity rule (live count: 0). 27 dedicated tests + 37/37 D9 regression tests + 63/63 Catalog #233 tests + 66/66 R2-CRITICAL tests pass. Sister of Catalog #237 (D9 fallback semantics) and Catalog #136 (custody validator concrete-tokens-only).
